campbell.title: Campbell City Council — May 19, 2026
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council held a meeting on May 19, 2026, where they discussed staffing vacancies and the city's efforts to recruit and retain employees. The council also addressed public comments on various city matters and noted that the Boogie Festival is happening this weekend.
keyTopics: Staffing vacancies and recruitment/retention efforts
Public hearing on government code section 3502
Boogie Festival this weekend
Public comments on city matters

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — June 3, 2026
saratoga.summary: The City Council met to decide which sheriff service options to adopt for the next budget year. After choosing their preferred approach, they planned to give staff direction on next steps for law enforcement services in Saratoga.
keyTopics: Selecting sheriff service level options for FY 2026
Council's near-term sheriff approach and priorities
Staff direction on implementation of chosen service model

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos Town Council — June 16, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos Town Council met on June 16, 2026, to approve meeting minutes from previous sessions and authorize the Town Manager to finalize several agreements. The council approved contract amendments and new service agreements related to software, legal services, and litigation support.
keyTopics: Approved previous meeting minutes from June 2 and June 8
Authorized software services agreement (five-year term)
Approved amendments to legal services contracts
Authorized litigation legal services agreement

san-jose.title: San José City Council — June 17, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council held a meeting to prepare for upcoming council sessions, reviewing and organizing agenda items for June 23 and June 30, 2026. The council approved spending city funds and accepting donations for the city's participation in the Annual Rose, White & Blue Parade and Festival.
keyTopics: Finalized agenda items for June 23, 2026 council meeting
City funding and donations for Rose, White & Blue Parade and Festival participation
Public records from June 4-11, 2026

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — June 23, 2026
mountain-view.summary: Mountain View's city council met to handle legal matters, approve the next year's budget and spending limits, and work on long-term plans for water management and climate goals. The council also celebrated local organizations and a city attorney's appointment to the judiciary.
keyTopics: Two pending lawsuits that could create significant legal costs for the city
Water shortage plans and 5-year water management strategy approved
Fiscal Year 2026-27 budget and capital improvement projects, including a new public safety building and golf course parking lot repaving
Climate and energy initiatives through a strategic partnership agreement
Salt pond restoration project in the South Bay area

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — June 23, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Sunnyvale City Council held a special closed-session meeting on June 23, 2026, followed by a regular council meeting at 7 P.M. The closed session portion of the agenda was not detailed in the public materials provided.
keyTopics: Closed session meeting held before regular council session
Regular council meeting convened at 7 P.M.

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — June 16, 2026
cupertino.summary: The City Council held a study session to review how parks and recreational facilities are currently zoned and designated in Cupertino, and to make any necessary corrections to align with state law protections. The meeting also included public comment time for residents to address the Council on matters not on the agenda.
keyTopics: Review of existing zoning for parks and recreational facilities
Zoning corrections to comply with state law
State law protections for parkland sites
Public comment period on non-agenda items

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — June 30, 2026
santa-clara.summary: The Santa Clara City Council held a meeting on June 30, 2026, using both in-person and remote options so residents could participate however works best for them. The city is continuing to accept public comments from residents before and during council meetings.
keyTopics: Hybrid meeting format available (in-person attendance or Zoom participation)
Remote participation via Zoom video conference
Written public comments can be submitted online through the eComment system
Phone dial-in option provided for remote participants

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — June 16, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council met to approve funding and development plans for the city. Key decisions included adopting a budget plan for 2026-2027, approving a new housing or commercial development project, and launching a crime prevention program focused on protecting storefronts.
keyTopics: 2026-2027 budget and community development funding approved
New development project approved with site permit and land map
Storefront Crime Prevention Program launched
City Council meeting schedule and minutes reviewed

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — June 23, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The City Council met to consider turning part of the Measure E development site into a public park and reviewed plans to expand the driving range at Baylands Golf Links. The council also heard updates from liaisons and departments on various city matters.
keyTopics: New park dedication on Measure E site
Baylands Golf Links driving range expansion study
Committee and liaison updates
